viable_jobs.title: Geospatial Technology Analyst Intern (Summer 2026)
viable_jobs.description:
JOB DESCRIPTION

FirstEnergy at a Glance
We are a forward-thinking electric utility powered by a diverse team of employees committed to making customers' lives brighter, the environment better and our communities stronger. FirstEnergy (NYSE: FE) is dedicated to integrity, safety, reliability and operational excellence. Headquartered in Akron, Ohio, FirstEnergy includes one of the nation's largest investor-owned electric systems, more than 24,000 miles of transmission lines that connect the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic regions, and a regulated generating fleet with a total capacity of more than 3,500 megawatts.
About the Opportunity
This is an open position with FirstEnergy Service Company, a subsidiary of FirstEnergy Corp. [SC00] As a participant in the Co-op/Intern Professional Development Program, you will be cultivating professional skills and performing professional level work. You won't just be sitting in meetings and shadowing all summer; you'll be actively contributing and doing the same work as other FirstEnergy employees on your team. You'll receive on-the-job training and challenging assignments. You will also be assigned a Supervisor, a Coach/Mentor and a dedicated IT Intern Program Coordinator to help you maximize your learning and growth throughout your summer... and beyond. Internships with FirstEnergy are full-time, paid opportunities spanning 14 weeks. You must be actively enrolled in an accredited university or college.
About the Geospatial Technology Analyst
The Geospatial Technology Analyst works with a team of analysts, administrators and architects in support of all things Geospatial related to Distribution (Dx), Transmission (Tx) and Vegetation Management (VMS) and other related applications. These systems empower designers, mappers, & third-party contractors for safe and reliable energy delivery. Team members apply software, hardware, networking, and/or engineering principles to support FirstEnergy's environment. The work focuses on maintaining a system architecture that meets the end-user requirements, while maintaining compliance with industry standards and regulatory requirements. Note: This opportunity is on-site and will work in-person four days/week. Position may be filled at Akron, OH; Greensburg, PA; Reading, PA; or Fairmont, WV locations.
Examples of possible job duties and responsibilities of an Geospatial Technology Analyst:
Understanding the business functions to provide optimal solutions that are scalable, reliable, and cost effective. Managing the testing and deployment of patches and software upgrades. Maintaining integrations and interfaces between FirstEnergy systems Performing system security administration tasks Supporting GSI DDS & ESRI ArcGIS for Designing and Mapping interfaces Supporting data extraction and transformation through the FME Workbench and Python scripts Developing data queries and extracts to meet customer requirements built on Oracle DB and Microsoft SQL Server Technologies Developing a solid understanding of processes in the FirstEnergy Operational Technology line of business, keeping up with developments in the electric utility industry at large, and being able to speak the language of the business.
Applicant Requirements:
Currently enrolled, full time, in a bachelor's degree program at an accredited university/college focusing on Computer Science, Computer Information Systems, Computer Engineering or other technology related disciplines Class Standing - Rising Junior or Rising Senior Maintain a minimum cumulative GPA of 2.5 Must have the ability to work without sponsorship (now or in the future)
Preferred Skills/Experience Basic understanding of, or experience with, any kind of coding or scripting language (e.g. Perl, Python, Java, C#, PowerShell, SQL etc.) Experience with relational databases, including architecture, design, and reading and revising data through software applications Experience supporting software solutions Experience troubleshooting personal devices,

viable_jobs.title: Data Science Intern, Summer 2026 (Onsite: Winston-Salem, NC)
viable_jobs.description:
Discover Your Future with Inmar Intelligence: Where Potential Meets Purpose

Are you ready to do meaningful work that actually makes a difference - not just another summer gig? At Inmar Intelligence, we don't just build technology, we power the modern marketplace. For over 45 years, we've been the trusted partner of some of the world's biggest brands, retailers, and healthcare organizations. From saving consumers billions to making healthcare safer and more efficient, our data-driven solutions shape the way businesses operate.

But what truly sets us apart is our culture. At Inmar, you'll experience a High Challenge, High Support environment that pushes you to grow - without going it alone. We believe in:

* Raising the bar in everything we do

* Seeking truth together, through collaboration and learning

* And putting our Customer First

Here, you won't just be an intern. You'll be part of a team that encourages curiosity, celebrates bold ideas, and gives you the tools to shape your career - starting now.

About this Opportunity

Inmar's Summer Internship Program offers a dynamic and enriching experience designed to give students real-world exposure to a professional environment while contributing meaningfully to company initiatives. Interns will split their time between a high-impact cross-functional team project and department-specific responsibilities assigned by their direct manager.

The MarTech Data Science team contributes to the design, development, and deployment of advanced data science and engineering solutions for Inmar's media and incentives solutions. We are seeking an intern that can bridge data science and engineering to enable scalable, high-performance analytics products and ad-hoc analyses that leverage data for actionable insights. The Data Science Intern will collaborate across multiple teams including data engineering, business intelligence, product, and analytics, to build robust data pipelines, deploy machine learning models, and develop solutions that power predictive analytics. You will have the opportunity (and the expectation!) to leverage enterprise-level AI tools to help you in these projects. If you are the kind of person who gets told a problem is "unsolvable" and your first reaction is "Ok, how would I solve it?", this is the team for you.

Please note: This is an onsite internship based in our Winston-Salem, NC location and will require in-person attendance.

What You'll Do

* Assist in building modular, reusable components for data transformation, feature extraction, and model deployment using modern cloud technologies (Google Cloud Platform).

* Contribute to the design and deployment of machine learning models and advanced analytics pipelines to deliver scalable solutions.

* Collaborate with internal stakeholders on the design of machine learning models and advanced analytics to deliver a range of ad-hoc solutions, with the ability to identify commonalities in requests and propose new products based on recurring themes.

Your Qualifications

* Currently pursuing a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Statistics with a concentration in Data Science, Data Engineering, or any related quantitative field with a strong emphasis on statistical programming and working with big data (e.g. Economics, Psychology, Computer Science, etc.).

* Strong analytical, organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills.

* Proficiency in DML (SQL) and cloud systems (Google Cloud preferred, but AWS, Azure, Spark, etc., are all great). You'll be asked to solve complex, novel data problems; we will have a large library of queries, scripts, and routines for you to build off of, but you must be comfortable developing independently from there.

* Proficiency in statistical programming languages and frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow/keras, PyTorch/torch) is required. Python is preferred for production-level code, but R or SAS experience is also great.

* Proficiency in using AI for code enhancement and p

viable_jobs.title: Global Social Media Data Analytics and AI Integration Intern
viable_jobs.description:
We are Lenovo. We do what we say. We own what we do. We WOW our customers.

Lenovo is a US$69 billion revenue global technology powerhouse, ranked #196 in the Fortune Global 500, and serving millions of customers every day in 180 markets. Focused on a bold vision to deliver Smarter Technology for All, Lenovo has built on its success as the world’s largest PC company with a full-stack portfolio of AI-enabled, AI-ready, and AI-optimized devices (PCs, workstations, smartphones, tablets), infrastructure (server, storage, edge, high performance computing and software defined infrastructure), software, solutions, and services.

Lenovo’s continued investment in world-changing innovation is building a more equitable, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere.

This transformation together with Lenovo’s world-changing innovation is building a more inclusive, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere. To find out more visit www.lenovo.com, and read about the latest news via our StoryHub.
About Lenovo

Lenovo is a global technology powerhouse, delivering smarter technology for all. As the Official Technology Partner of the FIFA World Cup 2026™ this summer, Lenovo is helping power one of the world’s biggest sporting events through innovative devices, services, and AI-driven solutions.
About the Role

Lenovo is a global leader in innovative technology, and our Global Social Media Team drives strategic paid media and analytics initiatives worldwide. As a Analytics and AI Integration Intern, you’ll support our team by automating data workflows, integrating AI tools for insightful analysis, and building interactive dashboards. This hands‑on role is ideal for students passionate about data analytics and AI applications in a business context, offering exposure to real-world projects without requiring advanced machine learning expertise. You’ll work closely with the team lead on connecting social reporting APIs to AI services, transforming data, and generating actionable reports to enhance content performance.
What You’ll Do
• Assist in building and automating API connections to integrate social data sources (e.g., reporting tools) with AI APIs and visualization platforms like DOMO or Power BI.
• Perform data transformation, cleaning, and querying with SQL to prepare datasets for AI‑powered contextual analysis on content and creatives.
• Develop interactive dashboard elements that allow users to query data and receive AI‑generated guidance for decision‑making.
• Collaborate on analyzing organic and paid social media performance metrics to derive insights and support global strategies.
• Hands‑on work with LLMs and AI integrations, prompt structuring, response validation.
• Contribute to documentation and presentations of project findings for the team.
• Lenovo works on a 3:2 hybrid schedule, 3 days in office and 2 flex. Our office is located at 8001 Development Drive, Morrisville NC. This role is open from Start: May 18 or June 1st through End: July 24th.
Qualifications
• Currently pursuing a Bachelor's or Master’s degree in Management Information Systems (MIS), Business Information Systems (BIS), Computer Information Systems (CIS), Data Science, or a related field.
• Basic proficiency in Python and/or Javascript for scripting and API integrations, and SQL for data querying and manipulation.
• Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, DOMO) and a general understanding of AI APIs for analysis.
• Experience with agentic AI automation and orchestration, especially with autonomous workflows.
• Experience with AI prompt structuring and validation.
• Strong analytical mindset with the ability to handle data structures and perform basic transformations.
• Excellent communication skills and a collaborative team player.
• Authorization to work in [relevant country] without sponsorship.
Preferred Skills
• Experience with API connections or automation projects (e.g., via personal or academic work).
• Knowledge

viable_jobs.title: State Street - Global Delivery - Alpha Operations Summer Internship 2026
viable_jobs.description:
Job Description

Who we are looking for

In this role, you will join the Alpha Operations team to work with State Street individuals from around the globe to support premier investment management clients. You will have responsibility to contribute to day-to-day Investment Operations in a fast-paced, flexible and exciting environment.

Why this role is important to us

You will be the first line of defense in surfacing any potentially risky activity, and will work collaboratively with your team members to ensure data accuracy. Conduct high-level analysis in specific areas of expertise, identify and resolve work-processing issues, make suggestions to streamline operations and implement. Join this growing high-visibility team focused on building innovative solutions to revolutionize how State Street services our customers.

What you will be responsible for

As Summer Intern you will
• Support the inquiries from clients, custodians and brokers to ensure timely effective processing of requests.
• Prepares, completes, and reviews standard client reporting.
• Performs transaction processing tasks within the business unit as necessary to support processing routines.
• Consults with peers to optimize the operating model.
• Oversees and trains cross-cultural team daily processing.

What we value

These skills will help you succeed in this role
• Working knowledge of Excel and other Microsoft Office applications and experience with Bloomberg is a plus.
• Highly self-driven sense of motivation.
• Naturally curious and passionate.
• Strong accuracy aptitude and detail orientation.
• Strong verbal written and interpersonal communication skills.
• Exposure to SQL or other coding languages is beneficial.

Qualifications
• Bachelor’s degree in finance, Accounting, Economics, or Business Management.
• Strong financial accounting knowledge and proficiency.
• Knowledge of Domestic and International financials; Middle/Back or Front Office Operations, Trade lifecycle mechanics, Fund Accounting and Administration, Custody/Accounting systems, Investment Operations, etc.

About State Street

What we do: State Street is one of the largest custodian banks, asset managers and asset intelligence companies in the world. From technology to product innovation, we are making our mark on the financial services industry. For more than two centuries, we have been helping our clients safeguard and steward the investments of millions of people. We provide investment servicing, data & analytics, investment research & trading and investment management to institutional clients. We genuinely believe our employees’ diverse backgrounds, experiences and perspectives are a powerful contributor to creating an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive and reach their maximum potential while adding value to both our organization and our clients. We warmly welcome candidates of diverse origin, background, ability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity and personality. Another fundamental value at State Street is active engagement with our communities around the world, both as a partner and a leader. You will have tools to help balance your professional and personal life, paid volunteer days, matching gift programs and access to employee networks that help you stay connected to what matters to you. State Street is an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer.

Salary Range:
$41,600 - $65,000 Annual

The range quoted above applies to the role in the primary location specified. If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ.

Employees are eligible to participate in State Street’s comprehensive benefits program, which includes: our retirement savings plan (401K) with company match; insurance coverage including basic life, medical, dental, vision, long-term disability, and other optional additional coverages; paid-time off including vacation, sick leave, short term disability, and family care respon

viable_jobs.title: Enterprise Operations 2026 Intern Conversion: HR Professional - Armonk NY Armonk, US
viable_jobs.description:
Your role and responsibilities This position is for intern to full time conversions starting January 2026 and later. By applying to this position, you are applying to the same role for which you were a co‑op or intern at IBM in 2025.

Required education Bachelor's Degree

Preferred education Bachelor's Degree

Required technical and professional expertise Successful completion of a 2025 IBM Co‑op or Internship

ABOUT BUSINESS UNIT IBM Corporate Headquarters (CHQ) team represents a variety of functions such as marketing, finance, legal, operations, HR, and more, all working together to solve some of the world’s most complex problems, help our clients achieve success and build collaborative work environments for IBMers.

YOUR LIFE @ IBM In a world where technology never stands still, we understand that dedication to our clients success, innovation that matters, and trust and personal responsibility in all our relationships lives in what we do as IBMers as we strive to be the catalyst that makes the world work better.
Being an IBMer means you’ll be able to learn and develop yourself and your career, you’ll be encouraged to be courageous and experiment everyday, all whilst having continuous trust and support in an environment where everyone can thrive whatever their personal or professional background.
Our IBMers are growth minded, always staying curious, open to feedback and learning new information and skills to constantly transform themselves and our company. They are trusted to provide on‑going feedback to help other IBMers grow, as well as collaborate with colleagues keeping in mind a team focused approach to include different perspectives to drive exceptional outcomes for our customers. The courage our IBMers have to make critical decisions everyday is essential to IBM becoming the catalyst for progress, always embracing challenges with resources they have to hand, a can‑do attitude and always striving for an outcome focused approach within everything that they do.
Are you ready to be an IBMer?

ABOUT IBM IBM’s greatest invention is the IBMer. We believe that through the application of intelligence, reason and science, we can improve business, society and the human condition, bringing the power of an open hybrid cloud and AI strategy to life for our clients and partners around the world.
Restlessly reinventing since 1911, we are not only one of the largest corporate organizations in the world, we’re also one of the biggest technology and consulting employers, with many of the Fortune 500 companies relying on the IBM Cloud to run their business.
At IBM, we pride ourselves on being an early adopter of artificial intelligence, quantum computing and blockchain. Now it’s time for you to join us on our journey to being a responsible technology innovator and a force for good in the world.
IBM is proud to be an equal‑opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, pregnancy, disability, neurodivergence, age, or other characteristics protected by the applicable law. IBM is also committed to compliance with all fair employment practices regarding citizenship and immigration status.

OTHER RELEVANT JOB DETAILS IBM offers a competitive and comprehensive benefits program. Eligible employees may have access to:
Healthcare benefits including medical & prescription drug coverage, dental, vision, and mental health & well being
Financial programs such as 401(k), cash balance pension plan, the IBM Employee Stock Purchase Plan, financial counseling, life insurance, short & long‑term disability coverage, and opportunities for performance based salary incentive programs
Generous paid time off including 12 holidays, minimum 56 hours sick time, 120 hours vacation, 12 weeks parental bonding leave in accordance with IBM Policy, and other Paid Care Leave programs. IBM also offers p

viable_jobs.title: 2026 Canon Insights Summer Internship - Logistics Intern 8
viable_jobs.description:
About the Role

2026 Canon Insights Summer Internship

Canon, U. S. A., Inc. is currently hiring for our 2026 Canon Insights Internship Program.

The program will be held from Monday, June 1, 2026 - Wednesday, August 5, 2026.

Canon is an innovative global digital imaging leader that uses technology to dissolve the barriers we
face in the connections we build, the experiences we create, and even the lives we live. We are
looking for passionate and experienced students to join the Canon USA team. This is a paid summer
internship 10-week robust experience based in our Melville, New York Headquarters.

What You'll Do:
• You will have the opportunity to gain hands-on work experience by participating in significant
work projects that help you learn, develop business skills, and provide a meaningful
contribution to Canon's business and goals.
• You will be exposed to world-class teams and our business, marketing, and strategy initiatives
through training, meetings, presentations, and other planned activities.
• You'll gain marketable skills and knowledge that you'll need in transitioning into the workforce.
• You'll come to the office 3 days a week and 2 days with the option to work from home (unless
a specific business need arises requiring in-office attendance on other days). This position
offers a hybrid work schedule on full-time (9 AM - 5 PM) schedule based on a 35-hour work
week. Note that work schedules and office reporting requirements may change from time to
time based on business needs.
Perks & Benefits:
• A beautiful Headquarters facility, which has consistently received awards for its many modern
amenities on a 52-acre campus.
• Free coffee.
• "Dress for Your Day" attire program - Casual dress, including jeans is permitted!
• Access to the on-site fitness center with locker rooms.
• A dedicated peer buddy who will serve as a mentor during your internship experience.
• Learning and professional development opportunities.
• Opportunity to network with top-level Canon business leaders.
• Exclusive training with industry leaders on Canon equipment.
• Volunteer opportunities serving our local community.
• Opportunity to participate in exclusive focus groups.
• A chance to be featured on Canon's social media sites.
• #CanonIntern Swag!
Award-Winning Culture:
Canon has a unique culture and is committed to our core values of integrity, mutual respect, fairness,
and communication. Our corporate philosophy is Kyosei - which we define as "all people, regardless
of race, religion, or culture, harmoniously living and working together into the future." This is reflected
in our welcoming, respectful, and family-oriented atmosphere with a strong focus on diversity &
inclusion, work-life balance, and community and environmental initiatives.

Your Impact

The 2026 Canon Insights Summer Internship - Logistics Intern will have the opportunity to gain hands-on experience across the Supply Chain environment throughout the 10-week program, the intern will contribute through a series of core department projects including:

Vendor Compliance Chargeback:
• Review/analysis/disputing, and reporting for major national retail accounts.
• Leverage analytical skills to identify trends and root causes.
• Perform data entry and analysis using Google Sheets.
• Create PowerPoint slides summarizing findings and highlighting opportunities to prevent future chargebacks.
• Provide feedback and recommendations based on completed reviews.
Supply Chain Projects supporting the U.S. network:
• Drive efficiencies across Distribution Centers and enhance customer experience.
• Participate in ongoing 2026 initiatives as timelines are finalized.
• Support project wrap-ups and assist with the launch of new projects throughout the year.
Supply Chain Environment Support:
• Assisting with group inbox management.
• Provide support internally within the company, while learning how various Subdivisions work as one.

About You: The Skills & Expertise You Bring

An enrolled unde

viable_jobs.title: Sales Data & Analytics Intern
viable_jobs.description:
Relay is the Intelligent System of Action for the physical economy. While the digital revolution has historically left the deskless workforce behind, we are closing that gap by transforming analog frontline operations into a data-rich, AI-powered ecosystem. We aren’t just selling radios; we are building the digital foundation for the 80% of the global workforce that doesn't work at a desk.

Why Join Relay?

• A Proven Winner: Join a hyper-growth company trusted by over 10% of the Fortune 500 and continuing to move up market with massive impact and success.
• High Value, High Impact: With 47% of our revenue coming from $100K+ ARR customers and a 135% Net Revenue Retention (NRR), you will be joining a company rooted in value creation.
• Market-Leading Innovation: The Relay platform captures 1 billion new data points weekly and features award-winning AI tools like TeamTranslate™, which has seen 500% YoY growth.
• World-Class Leadership: Our already stellar C-suite has been bolstered by leaders to help us scale toward our next major milestones.
• The Relay Culture: Work from our amenity-rich Raleigh campus (fitness center, sports courts) with 100% paid health insurance and a team dedicated to "Best Work In My Life" (BWIML).

If you're passionate about technology, driven to make a difference and align your work to a bigger mission, and excited to join a company that's shaping the future of frontline work, we encourage you to explore opportunities at Relay!

Role Overview

As Relay continues to scale and advance its go-to-market motions, the need for high-quality data planning, structured analysis, and clear operational insight remains critical. Reporting to the Director of Revenue Planning and Analytics, this internship provides an opportunity to contribute foundational analytical support to the Sales and GTM teams.

The intern will focus on hands-on data analysis, data extraction (SQL experience preferred), and prototyping AI-native analytical processes to support sales analyses & team execution. This role is focused on the data supporting the rolling 12-month horizon and will help ensure the right information is surfaced to sales and segment leaders by assisting in aggregating data, analyzing performance, and translating core data insights.

Success in this role requires the ability to perform analytical rigor, a proactive approach to data work, and clear communication skills.

Key Responsibilities

• Assist in Building Sales Planning Infrastructure
• Support the team by running data analyses to inform bookings targets, productivity expectations, and other modelling assumptions.
• Develop and run queries to update inputs for pipeline models, quota capacity, and other frameworks.
• Contribute to refining planning data as segments evolve and more performance data becomes available.
• Support Segment Performance Visibility & Analytical Analysis
• Use Hubspot and other database/SQL capabilities to extract, clean, and aggregate performance data across segments.
• Analyze performance data to assist with identifying indicators and emerging trends.
• Assist in diagnosing where execution is strong versus where it is breaking down.
• Typical outputs may include running Hubspot dashboards, SQL queries for analyses, productivity trends, etc.
• Develop AI-Native Analytical Processes
• Help assist in the design and prototype AI-native processes to automate data preparation, quality checks, or foundational reporting for the sales team.
• Help evaluate new analytical tools and methods to enhance the team’s sales capabilities - including sales efficiency and data modeling.
• Support Strategy Teams Through Data Supply
• Act as an analytical resource to the team by supplying the data pulls to help with structured insights needed to evaluate strategic options.
• Support the creation of sales operational models for segment business cases.
• Help quantify opportunity sizes and resourcing implications by querying source data.
• Help surface data-backed

viable_jobs.title: IT Business Analyst Intern (Summer 2026)
viable_jobs.description:
JOB DESCRIPTION

About the Opportunity
As a participant in the Co-op/Intern Professional Development Program, you will be cultivating professional skills and performing professional level work at FirstEnergy. Each participant will receive on-the-job training, and challenging assignments to refine skills and round out competencies. Summer Internships with FirstEnergy are full-time opportunities spanning 14 weeks. FirstEnergy offers paid internships to qualified students actively enrolled in accredited university or college. As an IT Business Analyst Intern at FirstEnergy, you will be on an IT team tasked to implement or maintain technology used to run an area of our corporate business. The role of an IT business analyst is to be the link between the technology and the business objectives. The business analyst listens, asks questions, analyzes and then documents what the team is describing they need. The business analyst knows exactly what is needed; therefore, the business analyst is critical to the successful testing and implementation of the technology. In this position, you will not be writing code. This position is for the analytical thinker who loves learning business processes and new technology. This position will be in the IT Corporate Products team and support corporate technology functions such as Finance, Physical Security, Flights Operations, and Compliance. Note: The opportunity is on-site and will work in-person four days/week. As such, you must be local to the Cleveland/Akron/Canton (Ohio) area
In this position, you will have the opportunity to:
Learn the business processes that you and your team are supporting. Learn the technology that supports the business processes. Work with your team gathering requirements for technology changes or implementations. Learn the business requirements so that you can develop or modify functional specifications. Complete unit, integration and regression testing for application projects or upgrades, ensuring that correct business functionality is being delivered. Use query tools such as TOAD, Power BI, and QlikView. Collaborate directly with the FirstEnergy business units, as well as other departments of IT, to complete and/or manage your assignments. Deliver effective client support through the investigation and resolution of client issues. Learn how to adhere to FirstEnergy Sarbanes Oxley (SOX) Controls.
Applicant Requirements
Currently enrolled in a bachelor's degree program at an accredited university/college with a major in Management Information Systems, Information Systems Management, Business degree with a minor in a technology program, or an equivalent degree. Class Standing - Junior (graduation date of December 2026 / May 2027) or Sophomore (graduation date of December 2027 / May 2028). Graduation date MUST be listed on resume Maintain a minimum cumulative GPA of
2.5. (GPA date MUST be listed on resume) Legally authorized to work in the U.S (without current or future sponsorship) Must be local to the Cleveland/Akron/Canton (Ohio) area Experience in Finance, Database management (oracle, sql) Experience in Microsoft office tools ( MS word, excel, outlook ) Excellent written and verbal communication skills Understanding and willingness to embrace IT application development standards and processes. Preferred Skills/Experience Experience in Finance, Database management (oracle, sql) Experience in Microsoft office tools ( MS word, excel, outlook ) Excellent written and verbal communication skills Understanding and willingness to embrace IT application development standards and processes.

Ideal Candidate:
Highly motivated to work independently and productively and learn quickly Must be organized and work well with constant priority changes Proven team player, contributes to team goals, encourages, and supports team members Strong attention to detail Willingness to undertake assignments involving unfamiliar subjects Highly motivated to work independently and productively, with

viable_jobs.title: Kearney Business Analyst Intern 2026
viable_jobs.description:
Description
As a business analyst intern at Kearney, you will work alongside a dedicated and diverse set of people and contribute to consulting project teams as we offer honest advice and practical guidance to our clients. With the freedom to be yourself and recognition for what you bring to the table, put your purpose into action and join us as we help build a future that works for everyone, everywhere - as a Kearney Original.

What to expect

• Gain unique hands‑on experiences as you broaden your knowledge and experience in an open, agile, and collaborative environment.

• Ensure the success of project workstreams by completing tasks such as gathering and analyzing data and conducting competitor assessment studies.

• Support in the creation of recommendations and implement co‑created solutions for clients across a range of industries and service practices.

• Take advantage of structured on‑the‑job learning and development opportunities, including a dedicated mentor who will provide guidance throughout your internship.

Who you are
After nearly 100 years, we know this business is fundamentally about making connections‑between facts, figures, insights, strategies, tools, technologies, and above all, people. That's why we look for collaborative, insightful, and inquisitive problem‑solvers who do not accept the first thing in front of them and who are always unapologetically themselves.

We want to hear from you if you are:

• Ready to share your ideas and contribute as soon as you join a team

• Analytically inclined and enjoy solving problems

• Able to prioritize and are a doer by nature

• In your final year of your master or undergraduate degree or fresh graduate

• Fluent in English and Polish

Interview process
If your application is successful, you will be invited to participate in our recruitment process, which consists of:

• Recruiting Test: 60‑min online test (similar to GMAT). Check out our Mock test available here .

• Interviews: We have two rounds of interviews. Each round consists of two case and behavioral interviews. Follow along with this simple guide and learn how to prepare.

Questions?
Contact

Equal Employment Opportunity and Non-Discrimination
Kearney prides itself on providing a culture where our employees belong and thrive equally, which means our people feel comfort, confidence, and joy as they do great things for our firm, our colleagues, and our clients. That's why Kearney is committed to building a diverse workforce and inclusive environment. Kearney is an equal opportunity employer; we recruit, hire, train, promote, develop, and provide other conditions of employment without regard to a person's gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, race or ethnicity, religion, age, national origin, disability, marital status, pregnancy status, veteran status, genetic information, or any other differences consistent with applicable laws. This includes providing reasonable accommodation for disabilities or religious beliefs and practices. We encourage everyone to apply, including those who may not feel historically represented in consulting.

general description

the business strategy & analytics intern will report directly to the manager of business strategy & analytics and will support all members of the ravens business strategy & analytics team. viable_jobs.description:
Summer 2026 Internship: Data Science

Location: Princeton, NJ

If you’re looking to grow your network and gain new skills within a global company, an internship with dsm-firmenich could be the program for you. Our summer internships provide students opportunities to work on real business projects and gain invaluable professional experience. As an intern, you’ll have exposure to many different areas of a global flavors, fragrances, and ingredients organization that works with some of the biggest brands in the world, along with opportunities to network with your peers and leadership. From engaging events to mentoring opportunities and valuable resume-building experiences, we’re committed to preparing you for a fulfilling and enduring career, both at dsm-firmenich and beyond. We are currently looking for a Data Science Intern at our Princeton, NJ office.

The 2026 dsm-firmenich Summer Internship Program will run from June 1, 2026 to August 14, 2026.

Working Hours: Monday – Friday, 8:00am – 5:00pm

Join our Data Science and AI team as an intern and gain invaluable professional experience. Enhance your skills and contribute to real projects that have visibility across the business through a Summer internship. Come discover all we have to offer!

Your key responsibilities

* Preparing AI-ready datasets (e.g. linked molecule data, harmonization of clinical trial related datasets, opportunity to design user-relevant clinical data management processes as part of an expert FAIR data), including feature engineering and data quality checks; conduct analysis, and exploratory research across active projects
* Train, evaluate, and fine-tune machine learning models under guidance from senior scientists
* Implement and test ML algorithm or small prototypes to support ongoing research
* Contribute to model performance reporting, experiment tracking, and clear documentation

We bring

* Build Your Future Skills: Gain hands-on experience and develop practical skills that prepare you for a successful career.
* Expand Your Network: Connect with professionals and peers in a company where sustainability drives every decision and action.
* Thrive in an Inclusive Culture: Join a community that values and respects every individual—regardless of background, beliefs, or identity.
* Grow Through Curiosity: Work in an environment that encourages open-mindedness, learning, and collaboration to create a better future together.

You Bring

* Currently working on completion of a Bachelor’s or Master’s or PhD degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Bioinformatics, Physics, Cheminformatics, or a related field
* Candidates must be available to work full-time on-site beginning on June 1, 2026 to August 14, 2026.
* Strong coding skills in Python and/or R, SAS, with experience using common data science libraries; Familiarity with cloud platform such as AWS, Azure for data processing or model development
* Solid understanding of machine learning fundamentals and experience working with real-world datasets is preferred
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
* Versatility, flexibility, and a willingness to work within constantly changing priorities with enthusiasm

The hourly rate for this position is $23.00 - $26.00 per hour. Compensation will be dependent on factors that include location, education, training, specific skills, and years of experience.

About dsm-firmenich:

At dsm-firmenich, we don’t just meet expectations – we go beyond them.

Join our global team powered by science, creativity, and a shared purpose: to bring progress to life.

From elevating health to making fortified food and sustainable skincare, the impact of your work here will be felt by millions – every single day. Whether it’s fragrance that helps you focus, alternative meat that’s better for the planet, or reducing sugar without losing flavor, this is where you help shape the future of nutrition, health, and beauty for everyone, everywhere.

And while you’re making

viable_jobs.title: Data Science Intern; Graduate Student - Summer
viable_jobs.description:
Position: Data Science Intern (Graduate Student) - Summer 2026

A little about us:

The Red Alpha Data Science practice grew out of Red Alpha's reputation in Software and System Engineering with our Department of Defense clients. As sometimes happens, customers who trust our expertise in adjacent areas asked Red Alpha to assist with some of their burgeoning Data Science problems.

Culturally, it probably suffices to say that we take our work seriously, but not ourselves. Our leaders have spent time in the trenches and have cursed daylight savings time changes and trailing whitespace as many times as you have. We like to say that we spend 80% of our time cleaning the data...and 20% of our time complaining about cleaning the data.

Joking aside, our voices matter, and it is easy to see how our decisions affect the Data Science practice and Red Alpha as a whole. We have a clear vision of where we are headed.

Our team takes a pragmatic approach to Data Science, defining it loosely as the intersection of technical expertise, business acumen, and soft skills to solve business problems with data. We spend a lot of time trying to understand the problem before we set about building a solution, and we prefer lower tech useful solutions over shiny algorithms and dust on the shelf.

Did we mention we’re pragmatic? We have a diverse set of skills across our team, and whether you are a traditional Data Scientist (whatever that means…), an Applied Research Mathematician, a Database Engineer, a Full Stack Developer, or something else in that neighborhood, if you have a knack for picking apart data to make sense of it, we would enjoy having a conversation with you.
A

day in the life:

The Red Alpha Data Science Internship (summer of 2025) is a thorough and pointed experience doing real-time, meaningful data science work. Throughout your time at Red Alpha, you will take on tough problems, get executive exposure, engage in out-of-the-box solutions development, and participate in technical challenges. There are no gophers here. You will be treated as a professional peer, because you are one!

Red Alpha invests heavily in the training, education, and professional mentorship of our interns. Each intern is paired with a mentor during their time with RED ALPHA to ensure they are engaged and immersed in work and experiences that will help them to grow professionally and broaden their technical tool set. From technology to business, we are looking for people who can innovate, collaborate and drive Red Alpha into the next era of an already rapidly – evolving digital marketplace.

You must be willing to live and work full‑time in the Columbia, MD area for the duration of the internship.

Work Hard, Play Hard, Succeed Together! That's Red Alpha. Join Us!
What you bring to the table:

Now on to the fun of formal requirements - we have to apologize in advance for the corporate-speak here, but just hold your breath for a few lines and everything will be okay. We are actively hiring data professionals for roles across a broad range of skill levels and projects. Our goal is to find the best fit for you so please note that if you apply to one of them and we see a fit elsewhere we will let you know.

So do not worry about applying initially for every position you might be interested in.
• You must be enrolled in a graduate degree program and majoring in Computer Science, Data Science, Computer Systems or a related technical discipline at an accredited higher learning institution.
• Experience with Python, Java, C++, R, and/or other related programming languages is required.
• Must have completed at least six (6) months of prior internship experience.
• Due to the nature of our work, customers we support and the requirements surrounding our office space, you must be eligible to obtain a TS/SCI with Polygraph level of clearance.
The total package:

Our total compensation package was strategically designed with our members in mind with the intention to: reward ou

viable_jobs.title: Summer Intern, Store Operations, Pricing Analysis
viable_jobs.description:
At Five Below our growth is a result of the people who embrace our purpose: We know life is way better when you are free to Let Go & Have Fun in an amazing experience, filled with unlimited possibilities, priced so low, you can always say yes to the newest, coolest stuff! Just ask any of our over 27,000 associates who work at Five Below and they’ll tell you there’s no other place like it. It all starts with our purpose and then, The Five Below Way, which is our values and behaviors that each and every associate believes in.

It’s all about culture at Five Below, making this a place that can inspire you as much as you inspire us with big ideas, super energy, passion, and the ability to make the workplace a WOWplace!

Five Below Summer Internship Program is ON! Our limited open positions for Summer 2026 focus on company strategic initiatives in the exciting environment of value-driven, fun-focused retail. We are seeking energetic, responsible go-getters with innovative ideas, ready to actively participate in company initiatives.

We LOVE our interns! Our internship is curated to deliver a rich learning experience for both personal and professional growth. Our program is hands-on, engaging in meaningful work that directly contributes to the company’s missions and ultimate success. You are an active contributor and valued as a part of your team!

Location

Our alumni agree – we’ve got the magic balance of flexibility and immersion when our interns align with our HQ hybrid model. All applicants should plan to be in the Philadelphia area and have transportation/access to HQ (aka WowTown) throughout the entirety of the program.

Internship Details
• 10 weeks: June 1st- August 7th
• Full time – 40 hours/wk
• Paid – $19.50/hr
• Hybrid model - Reporting into WowTown Tues-Thurs
• Hands on, results driven projects. No coffee fetching here!
• Strategic Project focused, coupled with day-to-day responsibilities and development programming

Highlights
• Interactive experiences with Senior Leadership Team
• Professional Development Sessions
• Lunch & Learns
• Insight and participation into companywide initiatives
• High exposure/small group
• 100% intern satisfaction rate
• High intern conversion rate! (On average, we hire 3-4 of our intern alumni each year)
• And of course – some fun product!

Project Details

Five Below is continuing to evolve its pricing strategy with a focus on making it simpler, clearer, and easier to execute in stores. This project supports our Pricing Simplification initiative, helping streamline price points, improve in-store execution, and create a more intuitive shopping experience for customers.

The intern will work cross-functionally with teams including Pricing, Store Operations, Merchandising, and Visual Merchandising to analyze current pricing structures and identify opportunities to reduce complexity. Through a mix of data analysis and operational planning, they’ll help translate pricing strategy into practical, store-level solutions—everything from clearer signage to more efficient execution for store teams.

Along the way, the intern will explore how pricing decisions impact both the customer experience and day-to-day store operations, identifying ways to improve accuracy, clarity, and consistency across locations.

At the conclusion of the program, the intern will present insights and recommendations that support simplified pricing across key categories, along with tools and materials designed to improve store execution and communication. Their work will help create a more seamless experience for both customers and store teams—making it even easier to shop (and work) at Five Below. 🎯

Requirements
• Must be actively enrolled as rising Junior, Senior, Grad Student or recent Grad with a relevant background; ideally studying Operations, Finance, Retail Management, Economics or similar. Those studying under an associate’s or certificate program may be considered if coursework and skills fit the individual proje

cpt_flagged_jobs.title: Data Scientist Intern - GenAI & ML Research (Summer 2026)
cpt_flagged_jobs.description:
Overview

LMI is seeking a Data Science Intern with a strong background in machine learning and research to explore the practical applications of generative AI. In this role, you will help design decision frameworks for large language models (LLMs), agents, and code-based approaches, tailored to specific use cases. You will also contribute to mechanistic interpretability research, ensuring that LLM outputs are genuinely grounded in the right source material.

This role is critical in developing ground rules for when to apply LLMs, agents, or code, assessing their strengths and limitations, and enhancing the reliability and trustworthiness of AI systems. The ideal candidate combines rigorous analytical thinking with creativity, technical curiosity, and sound judgment to address complex challenges.

LMI is a new breed of digital solutions provider dedicated to accelerating government impact with innovation and speed. Investing in technology and prototypes ahead of need, LMI brings commercial-grade platforms and mission-ready AI to federal agencies at commercial speed.

Leveraging our mission-ready technology and solutions, proven expertise in federal deployment, and strategic relationships, we enhance outcomes for the government, efficiently and effectively. With a focus on agility and collaboration, LMI serves the defense, space, healthcare, and energy sectors-helping agencies navigate complexity and outpace change. Headquartered in Tysons, Virginia, LMI is committed to delivering impactful results that strengthen missions and drive lasting value.

Responsibilities
• Develop a decision framework and ground rules for leveraging LLMs, agents, and code, including an assessment of their respective strengths, limitations, and risks.
• Perform mechanistic interpretability research to ensure that LLM outputs are genuinely grounded in reliable source material.
• Translate complex analyses into clear, actionable insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders, enabling effective decision-making.
• Work independently to explore and prototype novel approaches, while maintaining high standards of methodological rigor and skepticism.
• Stay up-to-date on cutting-edge technologies, frameworks, and best practices in generative AI and mechanistic interpretability to ensure solutions remain innovative.
• Collaborate with stakeholders to identify customer needs and recommend value-added solutions.
• Hone skills in consulting, project management, and cross-functional collaboration to support impactful project delivery.

Qualifications
• Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, R, or similar.
• Foundational experience or coursework in machine learning and data science.
• Strong critical thinking skills to evaluate models, assumptions, and data rigorously.
• Ability to work independently or on teams, balancing curiosity with methodical analysis.
• Effective communication skills, enabling the presentation of complex findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Desired Attributes
• Familiarity with commonly used machine learning frameworks (e.g., pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib).
• Experience with rapid prototyping, experimentation, and iterating on models.
• A creative yet practical mindset, willing to challenge assumptions while aligning with organizational goals.
• Knowledge of cloud computing platforms, such as AWS.

Other Information
• Interns will need to provide their own transportation to and from work and locate and arrange for their own housing.
• All intern positions at LMI are paid positions.

Applicants must meet eligibility requirements for a U.S. Government security clearance. Only US Citizens are eligible for a security clearance. For this position, LMI will only consider applicants with security clearances or applicants who are eligible for security clearances, due to the nature of the work.

cpt_flagged_jobs.title: Data Analytics & Business Intelligence & Process Automation Internship
cpt_flagged_jobs.description:
2026 Data Analytics/Business Intelligence/Process Automation Internship – Emerging Talent Summer Experience Program

Recruitment began on March 30, 2026

and the job listing Expires on May 1, 2026

Become an integral part of our JPMorganChase team! Each day will be unique – bring a positive attitude, entrepreneurial spirit and get ready to roll up your sleeves. This position is an essential part of the team and will have exposure to several aspects of running a banking office.

As a 2026 Emerging Talent Summer Experience Intern at JPMorganChase, you will be offered variety, growth, and a great introduction to a range of projects and tasks. With a heritage of more than two centuries, a broad range of core and alternative strategies, and investment professionals operating in every major world market, we offer investment experience and insight that few other firms can match.

The program is designed to provide a fixed-term on the job experience, and you will be expected to be available to work full time, starting from early June 2026 and for a period of up to 10 weeks. Roles exist in several areas across our lines of businesses.

Job responsibilities
• Provides general administrative support on key departmental day-to-day tasks such as document management, team governance, data maintenance and reporting
• Actively participates in projects from starting point up to completion and provides support to tasks relating to process documentation, automation, and simplification
• Collect, clean, validate, and analyze data to support business insights, identify trends, and inform decision-making
• Build and maintain recurring reports and dashboards; define metrics and ensure consistent, accurate performance tracking
• Partner with stakeholders to translate questions into analytical approaches and clearly communicate findings and recommendations
• Document data definitions, assumptions, and analytical methods to promote transparency and reusability
• Support process improvement initiatives by mapping workflows, identifying bottlenecks, and quantifying impact
• Assist with automation efforts (e.g., workflow automation, report automation, or low-code solutions) to reduce manual effort and improve controls

Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills
• Strong organization skills and attention to detail
• Ability to manage internal and external requests
• Able to prioritize and multi-task
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; ability to work with structured data and draw actionable conclusions
• Comfortable working with spreadsheets and data sets (e.g., sorting, filtering, pivots, basic formulas) and learning BI or Analytics tooling
• Ability to explain analysis clearly to non-technical audiences and document work in a structured way
• Curiosity and continuous-improvement mindset, with an interest in simplifying processes and reducing manual steps

Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills
• Demonstrated interest in Business Administration, Management, Governance, Finance, Economics, Wealth Management, Law, Accounting, Audit or similar
• Exposure to data visualization and reporting tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Qlik, or similar)
• Basic programming or automation exposure (e.g., Python, SQL, Alteryx, UiPath, or low-code platforms)
• Experience with process mapping / continuous improvement methods (e.g., Lean concepts) is a plus

Locations you can join:
• Jersey City, NJ
• Chicago, IL

Your exact office location maybe within proximity to one of our main hubs. This will be communicated to you prior to your start date.

Cohort Start Date: June 1, 2026

Become an integral part of our JPMorganChase team! Each day will be unique - bring a positive attitude, entrepreneurial spirit and get ready to roll up your sleeves. This position is an essential part of the team and will have exposure to several aspects of running a banking office.

As a 2026 Emerging Talent Summer Experience Intern at JPMorganChase, you will be offered variety, growth, and a great introduction to a range of projects and tasks. With a heritage of more than two centuries, a broad range of core and alternative strategies, and investment professionals operating in every major world market, we offer investment experience and insight that few other firms can match.

The program is designed to provide a fixed-term on the job experience, and you will be expected to be available to work full time, starting from early June 2026 and for a period of up to 10 weeks. Roles exist in several areas across our lines of businesses.

• Provides general administrative support on key departmental day-to-day tasks such as document management, team governance, data maintenance and reporting

• Collect, clean, validate, and analyze data to support business insights, identify trends, and inform decision-making
• Build and maintain recurring reports and dashboards; define metrics and ensure consistent, accurate performance tracking
• Partner with stakeholders to translate questions into analytical approaches and clearly communicate findings and recommendations
• Document data definitions, assumptions, and analytical methods to promote transparency and reusability
• Support process improvement initiatives by mapping workflows, identifying bottlenecks, and quantifying impact

• Ability to explain analysis clearly to non-technical audiences and document work in a structured way
• Curiosity and continuous-improvement mindset, with an interest in simplifying processes and reducing manual steps

• Exposure to data visualization and reporting tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Qlik, or similar)

cpt_flagged_jobs.title: Data Scientist Intern – GenAI & ML Research (Summer 2026)
cpt_flagged_jobs.description:
Overview

LMI is seeking a Data Science Intern with a strong background in machine learning and research to explore the practical applications of generative AI. In this role, you will help design decision frameworks for large language models (LLMs), agents, and code-based approaches, tailored to specific use cases. You will also contribute to mechanistic interpretability research, ensuring that LLM outputs are genuinely grounded in the right source material.

This role is critical in developing ground rules for when to apply LLMs, agents, or code, assessing their strengths and limitations, and enhancing the reliability and trustworthiness of AI systems. The ideal candidate combines rigorous analytical thinking with creativity, technical curiosity, and sound judgment to address complex challenges.

LMI is a new breed of digital solutions provider dedicated to accelerating government impact with innovation and speed. Investing in technology and prototypes ahead of need, LMI brings commercial-grade platforms and mission-ready AI to federal agencies at commercial speed.

Leveraging our mission-ready technology and solutions, proven expertise in federal deployment, and strategic relationships, we enhance outcomes for the government, efficiently and effectively. With a focus on agility and collaboration, LMI serves the defense, space, healthcare, and energy sectors—helping agencies navigate complexity and outpace change. Headquartered in Tysons, Virginia, LMI is committed to delivering impactful results that strengthen missions and drive lasting value.

Responsibilities

• Develop a decision framework and ground rules for leveraging LLMs, agents, and code, including an assessment of their respective strengths, limitations, and risks.

• Perform mechanistic interpretability research to ensure that LLM outputs are genuinely grounded in reliable source material.

• Translate complex analyses into clear, actionable insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders, enabling effective decision-making.

• Work independently to explore and prototype novel approaches, while maintaining high standards of methodological rigor and skepticism.

• Stay up-to-date on cutting-edge technologies, frameworks, and best practices in generative AI and mechanistic interpretability to ensure solutions remain innovative.

• Collaborate with stakeholders to identify customer needs and recommend value-added solutions.

• Hone skills in consulting, project management, and cross-functional collaboration to support impactful project delivery.

Qualifications

• Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, R, or similar.

• Foundational experience or coursework in machine learning and data science.

• Strong critical thinking skills to evaluate models, assumptions, and data rigorously.

• Ability to work independently or on teams, balancing curiosity with methodical analysis.

• Effective communication skills, enabling the presentation of complex findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Desired Attributes

• Familiarity with commonly used machine learning frameworks (e.g., pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib).

• Experience with rapid prototyping, experimentation, and iterating on models.

• A creative yet practical mindset, willing to challenge assumptions while aligning with organizational goals.

• Knowledge of cloud computing platforms, such as AWS.

Other Information

• Interns will need to provide their own transportation to and from work and locate and arrange for their own housing.

• All intern positions at LMI are paid positions.

Applicants must meet eligibility requirements for a U.S. Government security clearance. Only US Citizens are eligible for a security clearance. For this position, LMI will only consider applicants with security clearances or applicants who are eligible for security clearances, due to the nature of the work.

2026 UX Behavioral Science Operations Intern – Emerging Talent Summer Experience Program

Recruitment began on March 5, 2026. The job listing expires on May 1, 2026.

Become an integral part of our JPMorganChase team! Each day will be unique – bring a positive attitude, entrepreneurial spirit and get ready to roll up your sleeves. This position is an essential part of the team and will have exposure to several aspects of Behavioral Science teams.

As a 2026 Behavioral Science Operations Intern in JPMorganChase, you will be offered variety, growth, and a great introduction to a range of projects and tasks. You will work along side industry leaders in our UX Behavioral Science teams and gain insight that few other firms can match.
